subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus:
Grass clippings are placed in a bin, where they decompose. For 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 30, the amount of grass clippings
remaining in the bin is modeled by A(cid:11)t(cid:12) (cid:32) 6.687(cid:11)0.931(cid:12)t , where A

parts.prompt: Find the average rate of change of A(cid:11)t(cid:12) over the interval 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 30. Indicate units of measure.

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (a), point 1

parts.prompt:
Find the value of A(cid:132)(cid:11)15(cid:12). Using correct units, interpret the meaning of the value in the context of the
problem.

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (b), point 1
See College Board scoring guidelines for part (b), point 2

parts.prompt:
Find the time t for which the amount of grass clippings in the bin is equal to the average amount of grass
clippings in the bin over the interval 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 30.

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (c), point 1
See College Board scoring guidelines for part (c), point 2

parts.prompt:
For t (cid:33) 30, L(cid:11)t(cid:12), the linear approximation to A at t (cid:32) 30, is a better model for the amount of grass
clippings remaining in the bin. Use L(cid:11)t(cid:12) to predict the time at which there will be 0.5 pound of grass
clippings remaining in the bin. Show the work that leads to your answer.
